segments.text: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples:
The religious teachers and the Pharisees are responsible as interpreters of the law of Moses, [^144]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: so obey them and do everything they tell you. But don't follow what they do, because they don't practice what they preach.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They tie up heavy burdens, and place them on people's shoulders, but they themselves don't lift a finger to help them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Everything they do is to make sure they get noticed. They make themselves large prayer boxes[^145] to wear and long tassels on their clothes. [^146]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They love to have the places of honor at banquets and the best seats in the synagogues.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They love to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces, and for people to call them, ‘Rabbi.’[^147]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Don't let people call you ‘Rabbi.’ Only one is your Master Teacher, and you are all brothers.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Don't call anyone by the title ‘Father’ here on earth. Only one is your Father, who is in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Don't let people call you ‘Teacher.’ Only one is your Teacher, the Messiah.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The greatest among you will be your servant.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those who make themselves great will be humbled, and those who humble themselves will be made great.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But what a disaster is coming on you religious teachers and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You slam shut the door of the kingdom of heaven in people's faces. You yourselves don't go in, yet you don't let anyone in who is trying to enter.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What a disaster is coming on you, religious teachers and Pharisees, you hypocrites! For you travel over land and sea to make a single convert, and when you do, you make him twice a son of Gehenna[^149] as you are yourselves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What a disaster is coming on those of you who say, ‘If you swear by the Temple that doesn't count, but if you swear by the gold of the Temple, then you have to keep your oath.’ What blind guides you are!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What is greater—the gold, or the Temple that makes the gold holy?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You say, ‘If you swear on the altar that doesn't count, but if you swear on the sacrifice that's on the altar, then you have to keep your oath.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: How foolish and blind you are! What is greater—the sacrifice, or the altar that makes the sacrifice holy?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you swear by the altar, you swear by it and by everything that's on it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you swear by the Temple you swear by it and by the one who lives there.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you swear by heaven you swear by the throne of God and the one who sits there.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What a disaster is coming on you religious teachers and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You pay a tithe of mint, aniseed, and cumin, but you neglect the vital aspects of the law—doing good, showing mercy, exercising trust. Yes, you should pay your tithe, but don't forget these other things.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You blind guides—you strain what you drink to keep out a fly but then you swallow a camel!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What a disaster is coming on you religious teachers and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and the plate, but inside you're full of greed and self-indulgence.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You blind Pharisees! First clean the inside of the cup and the plate, so that the outside will also be clean.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What a disaster is coming on you religious teachers and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs, looking good on the outside, but on the inside full of skeletons and all kinds of rottenness. [^150]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You're just the same. On the outside you look like good people to others, but on the inside you're full of hypocrisy and wickedness.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What a disaster is coming on you religious teachers and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You build tombs as memorials to the prophets, and decorate the tombs of the good,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and you say, ‘If we had lived in the times of our ancestors we would not have joined them in shedding the blood of the prophets.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But by saying this you testify against yourselves, proving that you belong to those who murdered the prophets!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So get on with it—finish it all off using your forefathers' methods!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You snakes, you brood of vipers, how will you escape the judgment of Gehenna?[^151]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: That's why I'm sending you prophets, wise men, and teachers. Some of them you will kill, some of them you will crucify, and some of them you will flog in your synagogues, hunting them from town to town.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As a result, you will be held accountable for the blood of all the good people that has been poured out on the land—from the blood of Abel, who did what was right, to the blood of Zechariah son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the Temple and the altar.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I'm telling you, the consequences of all this will fall on this generation.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who are sent to you! So often I wanted to gather your children as a mother hen gathers her chicks under her wings—but you wouldn't let me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now look—your house[^152] is left abandoned, totally empty.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you this: you won't see me from now on until you say, ‘Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.’
segments.speaker: Jesus
